vulnerability.title: Configure AIDE to Use FIPS 140-2 for Validating Hashes
vulnerability.description: By default, the sha512 option is added to the NORMAL ruleset in AIDE. If using a custom ruleset or the sha512 option is missing, add sha512 to the appropriate ruleset. For example, add sha512 to the following line in /etc/aide.conf: NORMAL = FIPSR+sha512 AIDE rules can be configured in multiple ways; this is merely one example that is already configured by default.
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core; then if ! rpm -q --quiet "aide" ; then dnf install -y "aide" fi aide_conf="/etc/aide.conf" forbidden_hashes=(sha1 rmd160 sha256 whirlpool tiger haval gost crc32) groups=$(LC_ALL=C grep "^[A-Z][A-Za-z_]*" $aide_conf | cut -f1 -d ' ' | tr -d ' ' | sort -u) for group in $groups do config=$(grep "^$group\s*=" $aide_conf | cut -f2 -d '=' | tr -d ' ') if ! [[ $config = *sha512* ]] then config=$config"+sha512" fi for hash in "${forbidden_hashes[@]}" do config=$(echo $config | sed "s/$hash//") done config=$(echo $config | sed "s/^\+*//") config=$(echo $config | sed "s/\+\++/+/") config=$(echo $config | sed "s/\+$//") sed -i "s/^$group\s*=.*/$group = $config/g" $aide_conf done else >&2 echo 'Remediation is not appl

triage_decision.reason: The remediation only edits /etc/aide.conf to add the SHA512 hash and remove prohibited hashes. This does not affect authentication, networking, or boot processes and is reversible. The change is standard for AIDE hardening and poses minimal risk of system lockout or service disruption.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: filesystems affected; Potential misconfiguration of AIDE rules leading to false positives or integrity check failures
